name: Google SEO
body:
[{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"Since 2015, Googlebot can execute javascript files and read DOM. So, when Googlebot looking for our collection page, it executes each js file and builds a full page, that it can read and index. Therefore, there is no difference between a static page and a dynamic loading page, Googlebot will index them equally."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"There had been a few tests done to check the crawling ability of the Googlebot, here are the most important ones:"}]},{"type":"ol","children":[{"type":"li","children":[{"type":"lic","children":[{"text":"Test the search engine’s ability to account for dynamically inserted text when the text is within the HTML source of the page."}]}]},{"type":"li","children":[{"type":"lic","children":[{"text":"Test the search engine’s ability to account for dynamically inserted text when the text is outside the HTML source of the page (in an external JavaScript file)."}]}]}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"**Result: **In both cases, the text was crawled and indexed, and the page ranked for the content.
"}]},{"type":"blockquote","children":[{"type":"blockquote-line","children":[{"text":"📘 Articles
"}]},{"type":"blockquote-line","children":[{"text":""},{"type":"a","url":"https://developers.google.com/search/blog/2015/10/deprecating-our-ajax-crawling-scheme","children":[{"text":"Webmaster Central Blog"}]},{"text":""}]},{"type":"blockquote-line","children":[{"text":""},{"type":"a","url":"https://searchengineland.com/guide/what-is-technical-seo","children":[{"text":"We Tested How Googlebot Crawls Javascript And Here’s What We Learned"}]},{"text":""}]},{"type":"blockquote-line","children":[{"text":""},{"type":"a","url":"https://www.hobo-web.co.uk/javascript-seo/#can-google-render-pages-created-using-javascript-","children":[{"text":"How Does Google Handle Javascript When Crawling, Rendering & Indexing Pages"}]},{"text":""}]}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"
"}]}]
